sourceQuestion:
X is an online media provider. By offering unlimited and exclusive online content at attractive prices for a loyalty membership, X is almost forcing its customers towards its loyalty membership. If its royalty membership continues to grow at its current rate, within the next eight years more households will subscribe to X's loyalty membership than to cable television.

Which one of the following statements can be inferred from the above paragraph?

A. Most households that subscribe to X's loyalty membership will cancel cable television subscription
B. Non-members prefer to watch television and cable TV
C. Cable television operators do not subscribe to X's loyalty members
D. X is cancelling accounts of non-members
sourceAnswer: A. Most households that subscribe to X's loyalty membership will cancel cable television subscription

parsed.stem:
X is an online media provider. By offering unlimited and exclusive online content at attractive prices for a loyalty membership, X is almost forcing its customers towards its loyalty membership. If its royalty membership continues to grow at its current rate, within the next eight years more households will subscribe to X's loyalty membership than to cable television.

Which one of the following statements can be inferred from the above paragraph?

options.text: Most households that subscribe to X's loyalty membership will cancel cable television subscription
Non-members prefer to watch television and cable TV
Cable television operators do not subscribe to X's loyalty members
X is cancelling accounts of non-members

sourceQuestion:
While teaching a creative writing class in India, I was surprised at receiving stories from the students that were all set in distant places: in the American West with cowboys and in Manhattan penthouses with clinking ice cubes. This was, till an eminent Caribbean writer gave the writers in the once-colonised countries the advice to write about what is familiar and ordinary for them.

The writer of this passage is surprised by the creative writing assignments of his/her students because ______.

A. None of the students had written stories set in India
B. None of the students had written stories with ice cubes and cowboys
C. None of the students had written stories set in India
D. Some of the students had written about ice cubes and cowboys
sourceAnswer: B. None of the students had written stories with ice cubes and cowboys

parsed.stem:
While teaching a creative writing class in India, I was surprised at receiving stories from the students that were all set in distant places: in the American West with cowboys and in Manhattan penthouses with clinking ice cubes. This was, till an eminent Caribbean writer gave the writers in the once-colonised countries the advice to write about what is familiar and ordinary for them.

The writer of this passage is surprised by the creative writing assignments of his/her students because ______.

options.text: None of the students had written stories set in India
None of the students had written stories with ice cubes and cowboys
None of the students had written stories set in India
Some of the students had written about ice cubes and cowboys

sourceQuestion:
After India's cricket world cup victory in 1985, Shrutilk who was playing both tennis and cricket till then, decided to concentrate only on cricket. And the rest is history.

What does the underlined phrase mean in this context?

A. History will rest in peace
B. Rest is recorded in history books
C. It is a well-known story
D. History is archaic
sourceAnswer: C. It is a well-known story

parsed.stem:
After India's cricket world cup victory in 1985, Shrutilk who was playing both tennis and cricket till then, decided to concentrate only on cricket. And the rest is history.

What does the underlined phrase mean in this context?

options.text: Rest is recorded in history books

sourceQuestion:
In a press meet on the recent scam, the minister said, "The buck stops here". What did the minister convey by the statement?

A. He wants all the money
B. He will assume full responsibility
C. He will stop all enquiries
D. He will resist all enquiries
sourceAnswer: C. He will stop all enquiries

parsed.stem: In a press meet on the recent scam, the minister said, "The buck stops here". What did the minister convey by the statement?

D. She had a terrifying time at the party

sourceQuestion:
I do not think you know the case well enough to have opinions. Having said that, I agree with your other point.

What does the phrase 'having said that' mean in the given text?

A. as opposed to what I have said
B. despite what I have said
C. in addition to what I have said
D. contrary to what I have said
sourceAnswer: B. despite what I have said

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following sentence sequences creates a coherent narrative?

i. Once on the terrace, on her way to her small room in the corner, she notices the man right away.
ii. She begins to pant by the time she has climbed all the stairs.
iii. Mina has bought vegetables and rice at the market, so her bag is heavy.
iv. He was leaning against the parapet, watching the traffic below.

A. (ii), (i), (iii), (iv)
B. (iii), (ii), (i), (iv)
C. (i), (iii), (ii), (iv)
D. (iii), (ii), (iv), (i)

parsed.stem:
Which one of the following sentence sequences creates a coherent narrative?

i. Once on the terrace, on her way to her small room in the corner, she notices the man right away.
ii. She begins to pant by the time she has climbed all the stairs.
iii. Mina has bought vegetables and rice at the market, so her bag is heavy.
iv. He was leaning against the parapet, watching the traffic below.

sourceQuestion:
Sequence the following sentences in a coherent passage.

P: This fortuitous geological event generated a colossal amount of energy and heat that resulted in the rocks rising to an average height of 4 km across the contact zone.
Q: Thus, the geophysicists tend to think of the Himalayas as an active geological feature rather than a static geological feature.
R: The natural process of the cooling of magma absorbed a massive quantity of atmospheric carbon dioxide, altering the earth's atmosphere and making it better suited for life.
S: Many millennia ago, a breakaway chunk of bedrock from the Antarctic Plate collided with the massive Eurasian Plate.

A. QPSR
B. SPRQ
C. SRPQ
D. SRBQ

parsed.stem:
Sequence the following sentences in a coherent passage.

P: This fortuitous geological event generated a colossal amount of energy and heat that resulted in the rocks rising to an average height of 4 km across the contact zone.

R: The natural process of the cooling of magma absorbed a massive quantity of atmospheric carbon dioxide, altering the earth's atmosphere and making it better suited for life.
S: Many millennia ago, a breakaway chunk of bedrock from the Antarctic Plate collided with the massive Eurasian Plate.

sourceQuestion:

Q: Shifu said, "Surely my running away from the bull is also an illusion."
R: One day, when a bull began to chase Shifu, he began to run for his life.
S: Many millennia ago, when a bull began to chase Shifu, he began to run for his life.

A. SRPQ
B. SRPQ
C. RSPQ
D. RPQS

sourceQuestion:

iv. I decided I would rather wander down the passage.

A. (i), (ii), (iii), (iv)
B. (ii), (i), (iv), (iii)
C. (ii), (i), (iii), (iv)
D. (i), (iii), (ii), (iv)

sourceQuestion:
In the following question, the first and the last sentence of the passage are in order and numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the passage is split into 4 parts and numbered as 2, 3, 4, and 5. These 4 parts are not arranged in proper order. Read the sentences and arrange them in a logical sequence to make a passage and choose the correct sequence from the given options.

1. On Diwali, the family rises early in the morning.
2. The whole family, including the young and the old enjoy doing this.
3. Children set off fireworks later in the night with their friends.
4. During the day, the lamps are lit and the family performs various rituals.
5. Father, mother and children visit relatives and exchange gifts and sweets.
6. The children look so pretty with lamps all around.

A. 2, 5, 4, 3
B. 4, 5, 2, 3
C. 5, 4, 2, 3
D. 4, 5, 2, 3

parsed.stem:
In the following question, the first and the last sentence of the passage are in order and numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the passage is split into 4 parts and numbered as 2, 3, 4, and 5. These 4 parts are not arranged in proper order. Read the sentences and arrange them in a logical sequence to make a passage and choose the correct sequence from the given options.

1. On Diwali, the family rises early in the morning.
2. The whole family, including the young and the old enjoy doing this.
3. Children set off fireworks later in the night with their friends.
4. During the day, the lamps are lit and the family performs various rituals.
5. Father, mother and children visit relatives and exchange gifts and sweets.
6. The children look so pretty with lamps all around.

sourceQuestion:
Based on the given statements, select the most appropriate option to solve the given question.

If two floors in a certain building are 9 feet apart, how many steps are there in a set of stairs that extends from the first floor to the second floor of the building?

Statements:
I. Each step is 3/4 foot high.
II. Each step is 1 foot wide.

A. Statement I alone is sufficient, but statement II alone is not sufficient.
B. Statement II alone is sufficient, but statement I alone is not sufficient.
C. Both statements I and II together are sufficient, but neither statement alone is sufficient.
D. Both statements I and II together are not sufficient.
sourceAnswer: A. Statement I alone is sufficient, but statement II alone is not sufficient.

Statements:
I. Each step is 3/4 foot high.
II. Each step is 1 foot wide.

options.text: Statement I alone is sufficient, but statement II alone is not sufficient.
Statement II alone is sufficient, but statement I alone is not sufficient.
Both statements I and II together are sufficient, but neither statement alone is sufficient.
Both statements I and II together are not sufficient.
